findings.evidence: `Commutation.lean:135-142` repeats the same `(p₀ ∈ cIoo q₀ q₁ ↔ p₁ ∈ cIoo q₀ q₁) ∧ ...` pattern; `CyclicInterval.lean:46` shows `Grid.cIoo` is already the one-dimensional API this belongs with.
findings.fix: Define a one-dimensional `Grid.Noninterleaving (a₀ a₁ b₀ b₁ : Fin n)` in terms of `Grid.cIoo`, prove its `self`/`comm` lemmas once, then define the row and column predicates by specializing it.
findings.issue: `ColumnsNoninterleaving` and `RowsNoninterleaving` are two specializations of the same four-endpoint cyclic relation, with duplicated `self` and `comm` proofs.

findings.evidence: `Commutation.lean:78-103` repeats `G.O.toPerm.symm`/`G.X.toPerm.symm` and parallel lemmas; `Diagram.lean:134-138` already proves the underlying construction for any `GridState` as `x.toPerm.symm r`.
findings.fix: Add a general `GridState.columnOfRow (x : GridState n) (r : Fin n) := x.toPerm.symm r` with the apply/right-inverse simp lemmas, then specialize it for `G.O` and `G.X` only through notation or definitions if needed.
findings.issue: `OColumnOfRow` and `XColumnOfRow` duplicate the same inverse-permutation operation and its simp lemmas for the two marking states.

summary: The new API duplicates one-dimensional cyclic-interval and inverse-permutation structure instead of exposing the natural general lemmas first.
